snippet: /// Get the decay half-life for a namespace, falling back to the default ...

payload.action: Decide which default is correct and align code + spec + docs
payload.summary: Default decay half-life is 90 days in code (line 35: unwrap_or(90)) but spec S7.7 says 180 days. Also: doc comment on line 20 says 90, contradicting the spec

snippet: CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS spans (

payload.action: Add resolution TEXT and recency TEXT columns to spans table
payload.summary: Spec S7.8 defines resolution and recency columns on the spans table for freshness tracking. Neither exists in the current schema. Freshness types exist in damask-core but are not persisted

payload.action: Add CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE edges_fts USING fts5(payload, content=edges, content_rowid=rowid)
payload.summary: Spec S7.8 defines an FTS5 virtual table for full-text search over edge payloads. Not implemented — blocks damask search command

snippet: // Signal 1: Resolution (stubbed to 1.0 until resolve is wired)

payload.action: Wire freshness into signal 1, restatement check into signal 5. Signal 10 can wait for multi-source
payload.summary: Three ranking signals are stubbed to 1.0: signal 1 (resolution — needs freshness wired), signal 5 (signal density — needs restatement detection at rank time), signal 10 (source — needs community/local distinction). These represent 25% of total weight

snippet: /// Incrementally update the index — for now, full rebuild.

payload.action: Track file mtimes in index_meta table, only re-parse changed JSONL files
payload.summary: update_index() is a full rebuild every time (line 36: calls rebuild_index). Spec S7.8 specifies mtime-based incremental updates. TODO on line 34 acknowledges this. Becomes a performance issue with large JSONL files

payload.action: Wire into: (1) resolve command populates Freshness, (2) store persists to schema, (3) ranking signal 1 reads it
payload.summary: Resolution/Recency/Freshness types are defined in damask-core but never used anywhere downstream. Not stored in SQLite, not used in ranking, not populated by damask resolve. The types exist as a framework waiting to be connected
Spec defines damask search (FTS5-powered) and damask diff (show changes between commits). Neither command exists in the CLI enum. Also missing: --auto flag, --history flag, --resolved-only filter

payload.action: Add speed-check rule (edges/minute threshold), staleness-warning rule, namespace-schema rule
payload.summary: Spec S12 defines additional lint rules not implemented: speed check (edges created too fast suggest batch/hallucination), namespace schema enforcement, staleness threshold warning. Current implementation has 8 rules; spec implies ~11
damask review is defined in the CLI but spec S9.8 describes rich behavior: group new edges since last commit, rank them, show summary. Current implementation is minimal

snippet: /// Path to the JSONL file for a given namespace.

payload.action: Add is_ephemeral flag to namespace config, route ephemeral namespaces to .local/ dir
payload.summary: Spec S7.9 defines .damask/.local/ for ephemeral namespaces (gitignored, agent scratch space). edges_file() always writes to edges/ — no .local/ path logic exists

snippet: /// Truncated SHA-256 of span text (first 12 hex chars).
/// This is the content_hash stored on spans per spec §4.1.

payload.action: Add commit field to Span struct between content_hash and ns fields
payload.summary: Spec 0.8 adds commit field to Span for git-anchored resolution cascade. Not yet implemented. The Span struct needs: commit: Option<String> with #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]. This is the key structural change for 0.8 — enables 4-level graceful degradation via git diff/blame/follow.

payload.action: Extend damask-resolve to support git-based span resolution cascade
payload.summary: damask-resolve only computes content hashes. Spec 0.8 requires git-based resolution: given a commit:path:lines, use git diff/follow/blame to trace content to HEAD. This is a significant expansion of damask-resolve from a hash utility to a full resolution engine with git integration.

snippet: /// Path to the JSONL file for a given namespace.

payload.action: Design cross-project path resolution without over-engineering — keep the semantic web lesson in mind
payload.summary: Spec 0.8 §14.4 defines cross-codebase graphs — spans using package:path prefixes to reference dependencies (e.g., react:src/ReactFiberHooks.js). edges_file() and DamaskProject::discover() are single-repo only. Cross-project span resolution needs path prefix parsing and package-relative resolution (npm: node_modules/, pip: site-packages/).
resolution and recency columns added to spans table in schema.rs. Populated during index build via resolve_span().
Freshness types now wired: resolve_span() returns Freshness, build.rs stores to schema, at.rs reads resolution_weight from DB. Full pipeline connected.

damask-resolve now implements full 6-step resolution cascade with git2 integration. resolve_span() handles file-exists, content-hash, relocation, symbol, snippet, and unresolved. compute_recency() compares blob OIDs at commit vs HEAD.
FTS5 virtual table edges_fts implemented with external content mode and sync triggers. search_fts() query method added. damask search CLI command fully functional.
damask search and damask diff commands implemented. Also added: --all, --no-rank flags on at; --stdin on edge. Missing from spec: --auto (ephemeral span creation) and --history deferred to Phase 6.
damask review rewritten: git-based last-commit filtering, rank_edges() scoring, grouped-by-span display with score/agent, JSON output support.
Added stale-missing, stale-unresolved, and speed-creation lint rules. LintInput now carries resolution field from span data. 11 rules total now.

Resolution signal is wired in damask-at via Freshness::resolution_weight() (at.rs:138-153). Still hardcoded to 1.0 in damask-review (review.rs:71). Partially fixed — downgrade from stubbed to review-only gap.
Signal density now computed in at.rs:155-170 using token overlap penalty (1.0 - overlap*0.5). Still hardcoded to 1.0 in review.rs:72. Partially fixed.

Incremental mtime-based updates fully implemented in build.rs:70-128. find_changed_files() and find_deleted_files() track mtimes in index_meta table. Full rebuild only on first run or schema mismatch. Confirmed by commits 33ef996 and e51bb26.

Freshness is now wired: resolve_span() called during index build (build.rs:456), resolution/recency stored in SQLite, consumed in at.rs:138-153 via Freshness::resolution_weight(). Decay uses config half-life (180 days). Only gap: review command bypasses freshness (review.rs:71-72).
Code now uses unwrap_or(180) at config.rs:41. Test at config.rs:50-54 confirms default is 180. Matches spec S7.7. The 90-day discrepancy has been fixed.

Signals 1 (resolution) and 5 (signal density) are wired in the at command path. Signal 10 (source) remains intentionally stubbed at 1.0 (ranking.rs:87) pending multi-source support. Only 5% weight still stubbed, not 25%.

Incremental mtime-based updates fully implemented. update_index_with_mode() (build.rs:70-128) checks find_changed_files() and find_deleted_files(), returns early when nothing changed. No outstanding TODOs.
Span struct at span.rs:31-33 has commit: Option<String> with #[serde(skip_serializing_if = "Option::is_none")]. Tests at span.rs:82,119,133 verify round-trip serialization. Fully implemented.

payload.action: Compute resolution_weight and signal_density in review.rs the same way at.rs does (at.rs:138-170)
payload.summary: damask review command hardcodes resolution_weight: 1.0 and signal_density: 1.0, bypassing freshness and restatement detection that works correctly in the at command. Edges ranked via review have inflated scores compared to the same edges viewed with damask at.

payload.action: Support chained predicates or documented JSON filter form; update error text with parse hint and examples
payload.impact: Audit workflows require multiple sequential commands or external tooling to slice data, increasing triage time and error rate.
payload.reasoning: Repeated field attempts with multi-filter commands failed. Users building audit queries need composition (e.g. rel=risk AND tags contains security AND disputed=0). Current single-predicate UX forces shell piping through --format json | jq.
payload.summary: where command only accepts one predicate per call; users expect boolean composition (rel=risk AND tags=security)

payload.action: Show parse hint with 2-3 examples for common audit patterns (risk+tag+dispute=0) on syntax error
payload.impact: Slows onboarding and repeated use. Users fall back to --format json | jq rather than learning the predicate language.
payload.reasoning: When predicate syntax is off, failures give no guidance on correct form. Users waste cycles guessing syntax instead of triaging findings.
payload.summary: Predicate parse errors are not specific enough for quick fix; need parse hints and example patterns

payload.action: Split active into resolved/disputed/untriaged (or status + last_reviewed); make unresolved security risks the default view
payload.impact: Audit noise ratio is high. Triagers cannot distinguish genuine unresolved risks from stale findings, reducing trust in the graph.
payload.reasoning: Many rel=risk findings are already mitigated in code but still active from older checks. Failed validation looks like false positives because there is no way to mark findings as resolved without disputing them.
payload.summary: Stale edges swamp triage — no lifecycle state distinguishing resolved/disputed/untriaged findings

payload.action: Add batch dispute command, reason templates for common patterns, and clear undo/reopen mechanism
payload.impact: Manual one-by-one dispute is the bottleneck in audit triage. Users skip disputing stale findings, leaving the graph noisy.
payload.reasoning: Disputing stale findings one-by-one is tedious. No way to batch-dispute a class of findings (e.g. all risks in a file that was rewritten). No templates for common dispute reasons. No way to reopen a disputed edge if the fix regresses.
payload.summary: Dispute workflow lacks batch tooling, reason templates, and undo/reopen path

payload.action: Add native --tag, --namespace, disputes=0, and freshness filters directly to CLI commands (where, at, orient)
payload.impact: Audit ergonomics are poor. Users who cannot jq are effectively locked out of useful triage views.
payload.reasoning: Auditors need to slice by tag, namespace, dispute count, and freshness in a single query. Currently requires piping to jq, which breaks the interactive workflow and is error-prone.
payload.summary: Security triage requires shell filtering (--format json | jq) for useful slices; need native --tag/--namespace + disputes=0 + freshness filters
